name: King Slime
summonCondition: Spawn naturally in the far west or east of the map, or summon with a Slime Crown
strategy: King Slime is the easiest boss. He moves slowly and occasionally teleports. Use a flat arena and attack from the side while avoiding his teleports. He spawns Blue Slimes as he takes damage. Stay mobile and keep your distance.
tips: Build a long flat platform as an arena
He teleports when off-screen, so keep him visible
Blue Slimes spawn from him as he shrinks — don't get surrounded
Hermes Boots make dodging teleports trivial

name: Eye of Cthulhu
summonCondition: Spawns automatically at night once you have 200+ HP and 3+ defense. Can be summoned with Suspicious Looking Eye.
strategy: The Eye of Cthulhu has two phases. In phase 1, it hovers above and sends Servants of Cthulhu at you. In phase 2 (around 50% HP), it loses its pupil and dashes rapidly at you. Build a long platform and keep moving. Use wings or a double jump to avoid the phase 2 dashes.
tips: Fight at night only — the Eye retreats at dawn
Phase 2 dashes are faster and more dangerous than phase 1
Ironskin Potions and Regeneration Potions help greatly
A long platform with campfires speeds up health regen

name: Eater of Worlds
summonCondition: Summoned in the Corruption with Worm Food, or by breaking 3 Shadow Orbs
strategy: The Eater of Worlds is a worm-type boss. Attacking any segment deals damage, and splitting it creates more segments. Weapons that hit multiple segments (like the Vilethorn or Ball O' Hurt) are very effective. Fight in the Corruption near the surface for more room.
tips: Pierce weapons (Vilethorn, Demon Scythe) hit multiple segments at once
Splitting the worm creates smaller, faster segments — focus fire
Ironskin + Thorns Potions help for melee
The Corruption's Chasms make great natural arenas

name: Brain of Cthulhu
summonCondition: Summoned in the Crimson with Bloody Spine, or by breaking 3 Crimson Hearts
strategy: The Brain of Cthulhu has two phases. First, kill all 20 Creepers surrounding it — the Brain is invincible until they're gone. In phase 2, the Brain becomes visible and dashes at you erratically while creating illusions. Focus on the real Brain (it's the one that can be damaged).
tips: Kill all Creepers first — the Brain cannot be damaged until then
In phase 2, only the real Brain takes damage — look for the non-translucent one
AoE attacks are great for clearing Creepers quickly
Fight in a flat Crimson area or build an arena above it

name: Queen Bee
summonCondition: Summoned in the Jungle with Abeemination, or by breaking a Larva in an Underground Hive
strategy: Queen Bee alternates between charging, hovering and firing stingers, and summoning Bees. She becomes faster as her HP drops. Build a vertical arena or find a large open cave. Keep moving horizontally during charges and position below her during stinger phases.
tips: Honey gives a regeneration buff — lure her near honey pools
She speeds up significantly in later phases
The Beehive accessory from her drops gives permanent bee allies
Honey Potions are cheap to make and very effective here

name: Skeletron
summonCondition: Talk to the Old Man at the Dungeon entrance at night. Using Clothier Voodoo Doll if the Clothier NPC is present.
strategy: Skeletron has a head and two hands. The hands can be killed first to remove their attacks, but the head must be defeated to win. Destroy the hands first to make the fight easier. During the spinning phase (head spins rapidly), avoid contact and wait. Fight only at night — at dawn Skeletron enters a permanently spinning state and is effectively invincible.
tips: Fight only at night — he becomes invincible at dawn
Destroy both hands first to eliminate their attacks
During the spinning phase, keep distance and don't attack
Water Bolt from the Dungeon bookshelves is incredible here

name: Wall of Flesh
summonCondition: Throw a Guide Voodoo Doll into lava in the Underworld. The Guide NPC must be alive.
strategy: The Wall of Flesh spans the entire height of the Underworld and moves horizontally. You must outrun it while dealing damage. Build a long straight platform across the Underworld. Focus damage on the eyes (especially the laser eye). The Hungry enemies on the wall deal constant contact damage — kill them first. If it catches you, you die instantly.
tips: Build a 1500+ block long platform across the Underworld beforehand
Focus the Laser Eye for most efficient damage
Kill the Hungry first to stop their constant damage
Buff up with all potions before throwing the doll — no time to recover
Defeating this boss triggers Hardmode

name: Queen Slime
summonCondition: Summon with a Gelatin Crystal found in the Underground Hallow
strategy: Queen Slime has two phases. In phase 1 she bounces around the Hallow and fires gel projectiles. In phase 2 she gains wings and launches gel projectiles that fall like rain. She must be fought in the Hallow. Build a wide flat arena with multiple platform layers.
tips: Must be fought in the Hallow — she teleports back if you leave
Phase 2 wings make her significantly more mobile
Crystal Assassin Armor from her drops is excellent for early Hardmode
Multiple platform layers help dodge her gel rain attacks

name: The Twins
summonCondition: Spawns randomly at night in Hardmode, or summon with Mechanical Eye
strategy: The Twins are two mechanical eyes — Retinazer (fires lasers) and Spazmatism (fires cursed flames). Each has two phases. Defeat them both before dawn or they regenerate to full HP. Kill Spazmatism first as it becomes more dangerous in phase 2. A large open arena with long platforms is essential.
tips: Must defeat both before dawn or they fully heal
Kill Spazmatism first — its phase 2 cursed flame breath is devastating
Keep moving constantly — both deal significant damage
Daedalus Stormbow with Holy Arrows destroys them quickly

name: The Destroyer
summonCondition: Spawns randomly at night in Hardmode, or summon with Mechanical Worm
strategy: The Destroyer is a massive mechanical worm. It releases Probes that fire lasers when damaged. The Daedalus Stormbow with Holy Arrows is exceptionally strong here because the arrows hit multiple segments. Build a long sky platform to keep distance. Kill Probes as they appear to reduce incoming laser fire.
tips: Daedalus Stormbow + Holy Arrows is the fastest kill strategy
Kill Probes quickly — they stack up and their lasers overwhelm you
It cannot be split like the Eater of Worlds
Must be defeated before dawn

name: Skeletron Prime
summonCondition: Spawns randomly at night in Hardmode, or summon with Mechanical Skull
strategy: Skeletron Prime has a head and four arms: Cannon, Saw, Vice, and Laser. The hands are optional kills but removing them makes the fight much easier. The Cannon is the most dangerous arm. Defeat all arms first, then focus the head. The head spins like regular Skeletron — avoid contact during spin phase.
tips: Kill the Prime Cannon first — it deals the most damage
Head spinning is a safe phase to reload/heal if needed
Must defeat before dawn
Arms respawn if the head is still alive — prioritize the head after arms

name: Plantera
summonCondition: Break a Plantera's Bulb that spawns in the Underground Jungle after all 3 Mechanical Bosses are defeated
strategy: Plantera has two phases. In phase 1, she is tethered by vines and moves slowly, firing seeds and spores. In phase 2 (below 50% HP), she releases her petals, speeds up dramatically, and fires pink spiky balls. Build a large circular arena in the Underground Jungle. Phase 2 requires constant circular movement.
tips: Build a large circular arena in the Jungle — at least 100 blocks wide
Phase 2 is much faster and more aggressive — be ready
She must be fought underground — surface fighting is not possible
Bring more DPS than for mechanical bosses — she has a lot of HP

name: Golem
summonCondition: Use a Lihzahrd Power Cell on the Lihzahrd Altar inside the Jungle Temple
strategy: Golem is fought inside the Jungle Temple. It fires lasers, punches with its fists, and its head can detach and fly. Focus the fists first, then the body. The head becomes a separate target — kill it quickly. The fight is relatively straightforward compared to Plantera.
tips: The Temple arena is naturally enclosed — clear some space beforehand
Kill the fists first to stop their attacks
Once the head detaches, focus it down quickly
Beetle Armor's defensive set helps greatly for melee players

name: Duke Fishron
summonCondition: Fish with a Truffle Worm as bait in the Ocean
strategy: Duke Fishron is an optional but extremely tough boss. He has three phases. In phase 1, he charges and fires Sharknadoes. In phase 2 (below 50% HP), he spawns Cthulunados and moves faster. In phase 3 (below 15% HP), he becomes enraged: invisible, super fast, and devastating. Either burst him down before phase 3 or be prepared for extreme difficulty.
tips: Fight over the Ocean and build a platform above it
Phase 3 is extremely difficult — high DPS is essential
Fishron Wings from his drops are among the best wings
Dodge his charge attacks by moving perpendicular to his trajectory

name: Empress of Light
summonCondition: Kill a Prismatic Lacewing butterfly in the Hallow at night. Killing her during the day makes all her attacks one-shot you.
strategy: Empress of Light is a highly mobile boss with elaborate attack patterns. She fires beams, summons phantasmal spheres, and has sweeping sword attacks. For the Terraprisma (best summon weapon), she must be killed entirely during the day — but all daytime attacks one-shot you. This requires near-perfect dodge skill or specific cheese strategies.
tips: For daytime kill (Terraprisma), practice her patterns in nighttime first
Her attacks follow visible patterns — learn the tells
Endless open space is required — don't fight near walls
The Terraprisma is worth the effort for Summoners

name: Lunatic Cultist
summonCondition: Kill the four Cultists at the Dungeon entrance after defeating Golem
strategy: Lunatic Cultist has two phases. Kill the real cultist among the four first (attack them all — the imposters disappear). He then summons Ancient Doom, Phantasm Dragon, and Ancient Light. He creates illusions — only the real one takes damage. High mobility is key to dodge his diverse attack patterns.
tips: The real Cultist is the one that doesn't disappear when hit
Kill impostor Cultists quickly to prevent him summoning the Celestial Pillars early
Defeating him spawns the four Celestial Pillars
Ancient Manipulator dropped here is needed for endgame crafting

name: Moon Lord
summonCondition: Defeat all four Celestial Pillars (Solar, Vortex, Nebula, Stardust), or use a Celestial Sigil
strategy: Moon Lord is the final boss. He has three eyes: two on his hands and one on his forehead. Each eye has a core that must be destroyed when it opens. After all three eyes are destroyed, his core on his chest is exposed — destroy it to win. He fires phantasmal bolts, true eye beams, and summons True Eyes of Cthulhu when defeated. Constant movement and maximum gear are required.
tips: Destroy both hand eyes first, then the forehead eye
The Vortex Beater or Phantasm (ranged) are top-tier here
Keep a large arena — he spawns True Eyes of Cthulhu at the end
Buff up with every possible potion
The Nurse NPC can be used for emergency healing during the fight
